tooltips.centerAzimuth: Search Center Azimuth Point in degrees (ex: 50)
tooltips.azimuthExtentDeg: Total Extent Outside of Center Azimuth in degrees (ex: 4)
tooltips.azimuthExtentKm: Total Extent Outside of Center Azimuth in kilometers (ex: 120)
tooltips.centerElevation: Search Center Elevation Point in degrees (ex: 20)
tooltips.elevationExtentDeg: Total Extent Outside of Center Elevation in degrees (ex: 4)
tooltips.elevationExtentKm: Total Extent Outside of Center Elevation in kilometers (ex: 120)
tooltips.centerRange: Search Center Range Point in kilometers (ex: 1000)
tooltips.rangeExtent: Total Extent Outside of Center Range in kilometers (ex: 100)

links.buildOnObject: Build Short Term Fence on this object...

errorMsgs.stfNotInView: STF is not in view of the sensor!

help.overview: Short Term Fences visualize a sensor's search box: a wedge of sky defined by an azimuth, elevation, and range window. Operators use them to see exactly what volume a tasked search covers and whether an object of interest would pass through it.
help.imgAlt: The Short Term Fence menu with azimuth, elevation, and range fields
help.imgCaption: Define the fence by its center point and extents in azimuth, elevation, and range.
help.fields: The fence is centered on a point given by Center Azimuth, Center Elevation (degrees), and Center Range (kilometers), all measured from the selected sensor. The Azimuth Extent and Elevation Extent set the total width and height in degrees; the equivalent size in kilometers at the center range is computed automatically in the read only km fields. Range Extent sets the depth of the box in kilometers.
help.howToUse:
1. Select a sensor; the bottom icon is disabled until one is selected.
  2. Open the Short Term Fence menu (Create mode) and enter the center point and extents.
  3. Click Create New STF. The fence volume is drawn from the sensor in the 3D view.
  4. Stack additional fences by submitting again; Remove Last deletes the newest one and Clear All STFs removes every fence.
  5. With a satellite selected, the satellite info box gains a Build Short Term Fence on this object... link that pre-fills the form with the satellite's current azimuth, elevation, and range.
help.tip1: If the fence you request is outside what the sensor can see, creation is rejected with a notification instead of drawing an impossible box.
help.tip2: The kilometer extent fields update as you type, which makes it easy to sanity check how wide the search really is at range.
